title: Courts, Federalism, and Accountability
shortSummary: Learn how courts, federal-state relations, auditors, integrity bodies, and administrative law stop politics from pretending it is above the rules.
whyItMatters: Power without limits is not politics; it is domination. This module explains the legal and structural limits that make Australian politics a governed system instead of a raw contest of will.
coreConcepts: The High Court and constitutional interpretation
Why implementation can fail even when law is valid
mentalModels: Courts do not run politics, but they define legal boundaries
Federalism means power is split, negotiated, and often messy
Accountability is a ladder, not a single institution

goDeeper.label: Open the politics truth stack
goDeeper.description: See which institutions and authorities anchor serious political understanding.
